title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Language Clinic: Rag Release Canary Rollback
summary: A release has happy-path unit tests and one demo, but no component evals, injection or tenant tests, accessibility checks, load faults, rollback, deletion, restore, or stakeholder defense. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the release canary rollback increment in production RAG release and defense dossier. Lea…
objectives: Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.
Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-release-canary-rollback — Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_testing_release_defense_3(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

steps.title: RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-release-canary-rollback-nsnyq5" that uses case 3897 to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Release Canary Rollback into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback, keep Rag Release Canary Rollback connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-release-canary-rollback-nsnyq5
# Competency: rag-release-canary-rollback.
# Case 3897: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_release_canary_rollback_nsnyq5(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_nsnyq5 = "nsnyq5-5qynsn-3897"
    assert evidence_variant_nsnyq5
    evidence_axes_nsnyq5 = (
        "corpusnsnyq5",
        "query5qynsn",
        "tenant3897",
        "indexnsnyq53897",
        "prompt5qynsn3897",
        "evaluation3897nsnyq5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_nsnyq5)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Rag Release Canary Rollback structural check is only one gate. production RAG release and defense dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Release Canary Rollback precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6351 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.

For RAG and Python Build Rag Release Canary Rollback evidence · Theory Rag Release Canary Rollback, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Rolling back application code restores the previous RAG behavior.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.
